rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STEPHEN HEATH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Deposition Of Wendy Fung
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 19, 2016, Line 2, PETITIONER GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Wendy Fung The court adopts the substance of the judge pro tem's recommendation and thus GRANT the motion. Evidence Code §973(b) provides that there is no Evidence Code §971 spousal privilege when a married person brings a civil action for the benefit "of himself and his spouse." Stephen Heath and his spouse (Wendy Fung) seek to evade §973(b) with identically worded declarations that Fung supposedly "assigned, quit claimed, released, and surrendered" her "community property rights." However, no other information - any document, a date of the putative quit claim, any of its circumstances or other terms, etc. - is provided. I reject this tactic. To permit it would allow circumvention of §973(b); it would be far too easy to putatively abjure community property rights via lawyer-crafted declarations, only to reverse course after the time for deposition or trial. The sanctions requests are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/ru

rulings.Case Title: O. C. et al VS. HOUSE OF AIR, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Cross-Complainant House Of Air, Llc For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion For Summary Judgment Or Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 19, 2016, Line 17, CROSS COMPLAINANT HOUSE OF AIR, LLC's Motion By Cross-Complainant House Of Air, Llc For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion For Summary Judgment Or Adjudication The motion for reconsideration is DENIED. House of Air concedes that Code of Civil Procedure §1008 requires "both newly discovered evidence and an explanation for the failure to have produced such evidence earlier," but says this requirement only applies to "final" orders, and not "interim" ones. (Mot. 4:28-5:8.) However, the 1992 opinion House of Air relies upon was superseded by a legislative amendment that expressly applies the requirement to interim orders. (Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 688.) And House of Air has not provided a satisfactory explanation for its failure to have produced evidence earlier. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: CUPERTINO ELECTRIC, INC. CORPORATION VS. HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 19, 2016, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT The demurrers of City and County of San Francisco are OVERRULED. Transworld has pled present and existing actual controversies as to contract interpretation that may be the subject of declaratory relief. (CCP §1061.) Also, Transworld asserts third-party beneficiary theories not susceptible to demurrer. As to equitable indemnity, "an act that constitutes a breach of contract may also be tortious;" whether that is so here must await further litigation. (See Acadia, California, Ltd. V. Herbert (1960) 54 Cal.2d 328, 336.) Whether CCSF is immune under the facts here also must be litigated. (See Gov't Code §§815(a), 815.2(a).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RONALD JOSEPH VS. BUILD GROUUP, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 19, 2016, Line 6, DEFENDANT BUILD GROUP, INC, URBAN ATELIER, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. Under Privette v. Sup. Ct. (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689 and its progeny, general contractors are not liable in tort for injuries to subcontractor employees. (See SeaBright Ins. Co. v. US Airways, Inc. (2011) 52 Cal.4th 590, 598-600.) A narrow exception exists when a general contractor "retained control of the contracted work and failed to exercise his control with reasonable care." (Id. at 599.) Plaintiff has not met his burden of showing such retention of control, much less lack of reasonable care, as plaintiff's employer (not defendants) controlled job site safety for its employees. (UMF 1-20.) Defendants owed plaintiff no duty and there are no triable issues of material fact. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Declaring Tony Fu A Vexatious Litigant, For Security, And For Prefiling Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 19, 2016, Line 9, DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Declaring Tony Fu A Vexatious Litigant, For Security, And For Prefiling Order Defendant Yan's motion to declare plaintiff Fu a vexatious litigant is DENIED. First, Yan fails to meet his burden under CCP §391(b)(1) by showing that five litigations were determined adversely to Fu within the past seven years. As to CGC-10-501321, the Court's register of actions shows the case was removed to federal court and Yan provides no further information. In CGC-13-531993, both sides (Fu and Mr. Shen) dismissed their claims; the reasons are unclear. CGC-15-545811 was consolidated with CGC-14-539494. While Fu dismissed his claim in that case, Ms. Lei (who was aligned with Fu) recovered more than $140,000. CGC-15-549575 is ongoing. Second, Yan also fails to meet his burden under CCP §391(b)(2). While Fu has filed repetitive actions, Yan does not show that the actions are frivolous; a remedy is to seek consolidation. Third, Yan also fails to meet his burden under CCP §391(b)(3); he does not show that Fu's prior motions/ filings were frivolous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that he or she will not appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: PACIFICO SOLIS VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 19, 2016, Line 2, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Sigird Arias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties are encouraged to meet and confer prior to the date of the hearing regarding any mutually agreeable version of a protective over; the moving and opposition papers show that there is a disagreement about the form of protective order that would be appropriate, and the reply papers state that the moving party is willing to meet and confer further regarding the form of order. If no agreement has been reached, the court will hear argument on September 19, absent a joint request for a continuance of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Sigrid.irias@iriaslaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)
